metadata.title: Four hands-on tests of Kimi K3, GPT-5.6 Sol, and MiniMax M3
metadata.scoringNote: Each task is scored out of 100. Speed is listed separately and does not affect quality. The overall score is the average of all four tasks. Table order is Kimi K3, GPT-5.6 Sol (Codex CLI), and MiniMax M3.
metadata.methodNote: For each task, all three systems received the same brief and inputs on the same computer. The image test used the same 50 pictures. I tried every build in Chrome and recorded the runs.
metadata.publicDataNote: The site shows final scores, playable builds, and image-test answers. Run logs are not public.

2d.name: 2D web game
2d.prompt: Build an original browser game whose slingshot drag, release-to-launch action, and building-collapse objective are immediately understandable to an ordinary player.

kimi.note: The visuals, gameplay, sound, and complex physics were all there. Structures in levels two and three drifted while idle, and the mobile control area was small.

codex.note: Dragging, trajectory preview, collisions, reloading, and all three levels stayed stable. The visuals were simple; the controls and feedback were the clearest.

minimax.note: The opening screen, HUD, trajectory, trail, and Air Burst were all present. Vertical aiming was reversed, normal play could not clear level one, and progress disappeared after refresh.

2d.conclusion: Codex scored 96.0, K3 80.5, and MiniMax M3 54.5. M3 looked complete, yet normal play could not clear level one.

3d.name: 3D web game
3d.prompt: Build an original browser game that clearly communicates entering a 3D tactical training ground, moving and aiming, engaging enemies, clearing the area, and defusing a device.

kimi.note: The harbor scene, route hints, defusal feedback, and mobile controls were the easiest to understand. It also looked and felt the best. It took the longest.

codex.note: Combat, reload, damage, defusal, and results all worked smoothly. The scene was less memorable than K3.

minimax.note: Clearing enemies, approaching the device, holding to defuse, winning, and restarting all worked. Mobile controls and saved progress worked too. The visuals and shooting feedback were weaker.

3d.conclusion: K3 scored 91.0, Codex 89.2, and MiniMax M3 83.6. All three can be finished. The main differences are the visuals and control feedback.

promo.name: Promotion page
promo.prompt: Put the core appeal of One Kick directly into the page: visitors should be able to slide a ball on a compact 6 by 6 field, reason about the route, and score in three moves from the opening screen.

kimi.note: It had the most features. Route highlights, rules, game images, hints, and reset controls were easy to find. The opening was crowded, and the logo, characters, and field did not quite feel like one scene.

codex.note: The logo, field background, characters, and playable challenge felt like one match scene on both desktop and mobile.

minimax.note: The three-move challenge, real screenshots, and QR code were all there, delivered in 5 minutes 45 seconds. The oversized opening, brand feel, and scroll effects placed third.

promo.conclusion: Codex scored 95.0, K3 91.0, and MiniMax M3 85.0. M3 shipped fastest and placed third for brand feel and scroll effects.

vision.name: Image recognition
vision.prompt: Answer using only the attached image. Even if it is blurry, select the option that best matches the image and output exactly one line of JSON.

kimi.note: It answered 49/50 correctly. Of the 15 hard items repeated three times, 14 received the same answer each time. Dense counting was the only clear miss.

codex.note: It answered 47/50 correctly. Of the 15 hard items, 12 received the same answer each time. The misses were dense counting and a tiny distant arrow.

minimax.note: It answered 46/50 correctly. Of the 15 hard items, 12 received the same answer each time. Dense counting, tiny directional marks, and OCR without semantic context caused more errors.

vision.conclusion: Kimi scored highest on these 50 images, followed by Codex and MiniMax M3. The result applies only to this set and these three local tool setups.
